title: Fix load diagram interface and add text customization features

requirements.summary: Fix the load diagram selection interface to accurately display saved diagrams and ensure correct diagram state loads when selected
acceptanceCriteria: The load diagram dialog displays all saved diagrams with accurate metadata
When a user clicks on a diagram in the selection interface, the correct diagram state loads onto the canvas
All diagram elements (icons, connections, drawings, text labels) restore accurately from the saved state
The selection interface provides clear visual feedback when a diagram is selected

file_operations.description: Fix the diagram selection and loading logic to ensure accurate display of saved diagrams in the list. Update the click handler to properly load the selected diagram state including all icons, connections, freehand drawings, and text labels. Ensure metadata (icon count, connection count, last modified) displays correctly for each diagram. Add visual feedback (hover states, selection highlight) to improve user experience when selecting a diagram.

Verify and fix the integration between LoadDiagramDialog and the canvas state restoration. Ensure that when a diagram is loaded via the dialog, the complete DiagramState (including textLabels array with fontSize property) is correctly passed to useAttackPathState and all elements render on the canvas. Test that the dialog's onLoad callback properly updates the canvas with icons, connections, drawings, and text labels from the selected diagram.

requirements.summary: Add font size control to the text tool that allows users to select a font size before placing text labels
acceptanceCriteria: A font size selector is visible in the toolbar when the text tool is active
Users can choose from predefined font sizes (e.g., 12, 14, 16, 18, 20, 24, 28, 32, 36, 48)
The selected font size applies to text labels when placed on the canvas
The font size control is intuitive and clearly labeled

file_operations.description: Add a font size selector control to the toolbar that appears when the text tool is active. Implement a dropdown or button group displaying predefined font sizes (12, 14, 16, 18, 20, 24, 28, 32, 36, 48 pixels). Add state management for the currently selected font size and pass this value to the parent component via a new prop callback (e.g., onFontSizeChange). Position the font size selector near the text color picker for logical grouping. Use shadcn/ui Select component for the dropdown if appropriate.

Wire the font size selection from AttackPathToolbar to the useAttackPathState hook. Add state to track the selected font size and pass it down to AttackPathToolbar as a prop along with the change handler. Ensure the selected font size is passed to AttackPathCanvas so new text labels use the correct font size.

requirements.summary: Enable manual line breaks in text labels by allowing users to press Enter key to create new lines within text input
acceptanceCriteria: When entering text for a label, pressing Enter creates a new line instead of submitting the text
Multi-line text labels display correctly on the canvas with proper line spacing
Line breaks are preserved when saving and loading diagrams
The text input interface provides clear visual indication of multi-line support

file_operations.description: Update the text input mechanism to support multi-line text entry. Change the text input from a single-line input element to a textarea that allows Enter key to create new lines. Implement a different submission mechanism (e.g., Shift+Enter, separate submit button, or click outside to finish). Update the text rendering logic to properly display multi-line text with correct line breaks and spacing using SVG text or HTML foreignObject. Ensure line breaks (\n characters) are preserved in the textLabel content string. Add visual indication that multi-line entry is supported (e.g., placeholder text or helper text).

Verify that the TextLabel type and state management properly handle multi-line text content with newline characters. Ensure that when text labels are saved and loaded from the backend, newline characters are preserved. Update any text label creation or modification logic to correctly store the content field with line breaks intact.

requirements.summary: Update the useAttackPathState hook to manage font size state for text labels and pass the selected font size to newly created text elements
acceptanceCriteria: The hook tracks the currently selected font size for the text tool
Newly placed text labels use the selected font size
Font size state persists while the text tool is active
The hook provides methods to update the font size selection

file_operations.description: Add state management for the currently selected font size (default to 16 pixels). Implement a setter function (e.g., setSelectedFontSize) that can be called from the parent component. Update the text label creation logic to use the selected font size when creating new TextLabel objects. Ensure the fontSize property on TextLabel matches the backend DiagramState interface (DiagramState.textLabels contains TextLabel with fontSize: number). Export the selected font size state and setter function so they can be used in AttackPathPage and passed to the toolbar.
